sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.69 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: For every finite simple group find the minimum of the number of generating involutions satisfying an additional condition, in each of the following cases. [r]{ a)} The product of the generating involutions equals~$1$. [r]{b)} (Malle--Saxl--Weigel). All generating involutions are conjugate. [r]{c)} (Malle--Saxl--Weigel). The conditions a) and b) are simultaneously satisfied. Editors' comment: parti...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
